title: Freezing of hPSC
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"aqvhg","text":"This protocol describes cryopreservation of hPSC as clumps or single cells.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"8hn61","text":"hPSC culture quality is highly relevant for a successful freezing/thawing procedure. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ecsi8","text":"We recommend freezing cultures at ~70-80% confluency. High quality cultures show spontaneous differentiation below 5-10% of cultured surface. steps: Aspirate and discard medium from the vessel.
Add required volume of non-enzymatic dissociation reagent to the culture vessel, refer to Table 1 for recommended volumes.
Gently aspirate non-enzymatic dissociation reagent gently without disturbing the cells and discard.
Gently tap the plate 00:00:05, - 00:00:10 to dislodge the cells from the plastic surface. Gently scraping the vessel surface aids in clump harvesting when plate tapping alone is insufficient.
Immediately place cryo-vials into the freezing container and place at -80 °C Overnight When available, controlled freezing using automated devices e.g. Viafreeze (Cytiva) is recommended.
Transfer hPSC containing cryo-vials to a liquid N 2 tank after 24:00:00
Add required volume of freezing medium to the culture vessel, refer to Table 1 for recommended volumes.

Use a 2 mL pipette to transfer hPSC suspension (ideally 1 mL ) into pre-labeled cryo-vials. If multiple wells or vessels from same culture are going to be frozen, hPSC suspensions have to bepooled and homogenize in a 15 or 50 mL tube before freezing. Thus, every cryo-vial contains equal material.
Choose freezing method:
Visually inspect hPSC culture using a microscope. Culture ready for freezing should be of good quality (see Guidelines section).

Rinse once with required volume of non-enzymatic dissociation reagent, refer to Table 1 for recommended volumes. A variety of non-enzymatic dissociation reagents can be used. For options refer to protocol: Non-enzymatic passaging of hPSC
Incubate for 00:03:00, - 00:05:00 at Room temperature . Monitor under microscope. When hPSC colonies look losen, stop the incubation.

Refer to Table 1 to prepare adequate amount of dissociation reagent and freezing medium according to the culture vessel format and amount of vessels/wells to be frozen. A B C D 1 Culture vessel Dissociation reagent [mL] Cryopreservation media [mL] Re-suspension media [mL] 2 24 well 0.25 0.25 0.5 3 12 well 0.5 0.5 1 4 6 well 1 1 2 5 T25 3 3 6 6 T75 6 10 10 7 10 cm dish 3 10 10 Table 1. Recommended volume according to vessel format Freezing as single cells additionally requires the use of re-suspension media (culture media supplemented with survival factors as described in protocol: Survival factors for hPSC growth ) to wash out enzymatic dissociation reagent. Dissociation reagent will depend on the freezing method choice: as clumps or as single cells see STEP CASE. Freezing medium options are described in material and methods, we recommend the use of BAMBANKER BioCat GmbH Catalog #BB03-NP
Aspirate and discard medium from the vessels.

Add required volume of enzymatic dissociation reagent, refer to Table 1 for recommended volumes . A variety of enzymatic dissociation reagents can be used. For options refer to protocol Single cell passaging of hPSC

Incubate for 00:05:00, - 00:10:00 at 37°C. Monitor the cells under a microscope. When hPSC colonies look losen, tap the vessel from the sides. If hPSC are in suspension, stop incubation. If hPSC are still attached leave an additional minute and repeat the process.
Collect hPSC suspension in 15 or 50 mL tube containing 5 mL hPSC culture media and rinse wells/vessels with additional culture medium, refer to Table 1 for recommended volumes.
Centrifuge hPSC suspension at 300 x g, °C, for 00:05:00
Aspirate and discard supernatant.

Re-suspend cells in freezing medium and perform hPSC counting. Volume re-suspension depends on number of cryo-vials to be frozen. We recommend a range of 0.8-2 million cells per cryo-vial, freezing volume 1 mL
Distribute homogeneously among cryo-vials hPSC suspension.
Immediately place cryo-vials into the freezing container and store at -80 °C When available, controlled freezing using automated devices e.g. Viafreeze (Cytiva) is recommended.
Transfer hPSC containing cryo-vials to a liquid N 2 tank after 24:00:00
creator: Harald Stachelscheid
